column1: # SR2 core Equipment Table ("Street Gear", book p.254+) — the CANONICAL price
# list (see CLAUDE.md). Transcribed from rendered PDF pages, not the text layer:
# the text layer mis-aligns these columns badly. Where the combat chapter prints
# a different value, THIS table wins.
# damage "" = not a weapon / not applicable. type: p=physical, s=stun.
# bookPage is the PRINTED page (the footer folio), not a PDF page: the offset
# is not constant across the book. Verified against the corrected 11th printing.
# Assault rifles / heavy weapons (book p.255). Names are OURS where they differ from
# the printed row: Panther Assault Cannon = "Assault Cannon", Light Machine Gun
# Clothing & armor + commercial explosives (book p.257). `extra` carries the
# Read off rendered pages: the text layer mangles this section's headers
# ("Avallablllty", "Saeet Index"). The Essence column prints a division glyph
# that both the scan and the text layer render as "+": "Rating + 10" is
# mechanically impossible (a character has 6 Essence), so it is Rating ÷ 10 —
# which is what the per-rating tables already implement. Rating-table items
# (Filtration, Skillwires, Muscle Replacement, Dermal Plating, Vehicle Control
# Rig) carry their values in `ratingStats`, so only their flat/derived values
# are checked here; Vehicle Control Rig is omitted because its Street Index
# differs per level (1 / 1.25 / 1.5) and is set on its rating rows instead.
# Cyberlimb options (p.261) price as ADDITIONS to the parent limb. Two are
# formulas and stay formulas: 150000*rating (Increased Strength) and 4*base
# (Built-In Device = 4 x the device's normal cost).
